explanation: SAM database access. The Security Account Manager stores Windows password hashes. Access indicates critical file read capability.
Two-level SAM file traversal with Windows backslash separators. Tests for backslash path traversal support.
Three-level SAM traversal. Covers typical IIS deployment depths.
Four-level SAM access for deeper application directory structures.
Classic Windows test file. win.ini always exists and is world-readable. A reliable indicator of path traversal without elevated privileges.
Two-level win.ini traversal. A safe test target that confirms vulnerability without accessing sensitive data.
Three-level win.ini access. Standard depth for IIS applications deployed under wwwroot.
Four-level win.ini access. Covers deeper deployment paths.
System.ini file. Another classic test target that always exists on Windows systems.
Two-level system.ini traversal. Alternative to win.ini for confirming file read.
Boot configuration file. Present on older Windows systems (XP/2003). Reveals OS version and boot partition layout.
Two-level boot.ini traversal.
Windows hosts file. Contains DNS override entries revealing internal hostnames and IP addresses.
Two-level hosts file access with backslash separators.
Three-level Windows hosts file traversal.
SAM repair backup. The repair directory contains backup copies of the SAM database, sometimes with weaker permissions.
SYSTEM registry hive backup. Contains the key needed to decrypt SAM password hashes.
IIS log files directory. Access log files may contain sensitive URL parameters and authentication tokens.
Absolute path to win.ini. Tests if the application allows absolute Windows paths directly.
Absolute system.ini path. Tests absolute path access without traversal sequences.
Absolute boot.ini path for legacy Windows systems.
Absolute SAM path. Direct access without traversal.
Absolute hosts file path. Bypasses ../ filtering entirely.
IIS web.config file. Contains connection strings, authentication settings, and application configuration including potential database credentials.
Domain join log. Contains the domain name and domain controller information. Useful for Active Directory reconnaissance.
